mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand that forces corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive forward returns in greater than 50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of contraction episodes or the signal loses predictive power during non-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation remains above 300 while the federal funds rate stays below 5%, markets price in delayed monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive returns in greater than 60% of observations or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 50% during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5% signals economic stagnation that reduces equity risk premiums and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of low-growth quarters or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and stagnant payroll growth indicates capital-intensive expansion that boosts corporate margins and drives positive short-term equity returns during early-cycle recoveries.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields negative returns in greater than 60% of cases or loses predictive significance outside early-cycle regimes.
